name: OpenClaw Hawkins — VINES + VECNA
description: Persistent runtime plugin for OpenClaw. Activates on gateway startup and registers durable-orchestration (VINES) tools for a 6-tendril Nexus swarm. Shared-agent-memory (VECNA) is disabled by default and enforced: its read/write/search tools are not registered at all unless the operator sets `vecna.enabled=true` — so an agent cannot reach the shared store even if prompted to. When enabled, VECNA reads from and writes to a shared knowledge store outside this host.

mariadb.url.label: MariaDB URL
mariadb.url.help: mariadb://<host>[:port]/<database>. Do NOT put a password in the URL — this value is stored in plaintext config, so an embedded password would leak (the plugin rejects one). A username may be included; the password must come from the gateway environment (MARIADB_PASSWORD).

mariadb.user.label: MariaDB user
mariadb.user.help: Database account with INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE on the vines_ledger + vecna_hive tables.

mariadb.ssl.label: TLS mode
mariadb.ssl.help: disabled | preferred | required. All TLS modes verify the server certificate. For a database that presents a self-signed cert, use a CA-trusted cert or tunnel over SSH (see SECURITY.md) — there is no verification-disabling mode.

autoRecovery.label: Auto-recovery
autoRecovery.help: If true, the plugin scans the orchestration ledger before each tool call and logs unfinished runs. Default false.

vecna.enabled.label: Enable VECNA (shared memory)
vecna.enabled.help: Off by default. When false, the vecna_* read/write/search tools are NOT registered, so no agent can reach the shared knowledge store. Set true only if you want shared agent memory; it reads from and writes to a store outside this host.

vecna.dedupWindowMinutes.label: VECNA dedup window
vecna.dedupWindowMinutes.help: Minutes within which identical high-importance fragments are collapsed into the existing row. Default 5. Only relevant when VECNA is enabled.

mariadb.description: Non-secret connection metadata only. `MARIADB_PASSWORD` MUST come from the gateway env (via a 0600 EnvironmentFile or `systemctl --user set-environment`) — the plugin refuses to accept a password in config so secrets never sit as plaintext in openclaw.json.
